mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the year-over-year change of consumer prices (inflation) signals eroding corporate profit margins and anticipates future monetary tightening by the central bank, leading to a higher discount rate for equities and thus negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard economic theory links higher inflation to tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient between CPIAUCSL YoY % change and 60-day forward returns remains positive for any continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month period is a strong real-time indicator of a cyclical downturn, reducing expected corporate earnings and consumer demand, thus negatively impacting equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts a positive 20-day forward return, indicating the market is ignoring the labor market signal.

mechanism_narrative: When strong retail sales growth coincides with a contraction in housing starts, it signals an unhealthy economic divergence where consumer spending is not supported by wealth effects from the housing market. This unsustainable pattern predicts a near-term market correction.
failure_condition: A regression model including the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST growth fails to show a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production indicates that supply chains are normalizing and corporate revenues are expanding robustly, driving positive equity returns as the economy regains its footing.
evidence_refs: fact:The economy as of 2021-02-15 is in a recovery phase from the 2020 COVID-19 shock.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly changes and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative during the 24 months following an NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in nonfarm payrolls growth directly reflects a robust labor market, which supports aggregate demand through higher consumer spending and boosts investor sentiment, leading to positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: The beta of 20-day forward returns on the 1-month change in PAYEMS is not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and decelerating real GDP growth signals a stagflationary environment. This simultaneously compresses corporate profit margins via input costs and increases the equity risk premium, leading to negative market returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3% AND GDPC1 QoQ change < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation of the 1970s was associated with poor equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 average 60-day forward return is positive during periods when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts, a key leading indicator, signals an imminent contraction in a major cyclical sector. This negatively impacts investor sentiment and expectations for broad economic activity, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly lower than the average return on non-trigger days (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is already at a restrictive level, any decrease signals a policy pivot towards easing. This provides significant relief to corporate borrowing costs and lowers the discount rate for future earnings, acting as a powerful positive catalyst for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:This mechanism is not currently active as FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: When the FEDFUNDS level is above 3%, the correlation between monthly decreases in the rate and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year period.
